title: Binary Tree Preorder Traversal
hint: Visit root first, then left subtree, then right subtree.
intuition: DFS recursively explores depth before breadth.

title: Binary Tree Inorder Traversal
hint: Visit left subtree, root, then right subtree.
intuition: Inorder traversal of BST gives sorted order.

title: Binary Tree Postorder Traversal
hint: Visit children before the root.
intuition: Postorder processes dependencies before parent.

title: Maximum Depth of Binary Tree
hint: Depth is 1 + max(leftDepth, rightDepth).
intuition: Tree height depends on deepest subtree.

title: Balanced Binary Tree
hint: Return height while checking balance simultaneously.
intuition: Bottom-up DFS avoids repeated height calculations.

title: Same Tree
hint: Compare node values and both subtrees recursively.
intuition: Trees are equal only if structure and values match.

title: Symmetric Tree
hint: Compare left subtree with mirrored right subtree.
intuition: Mirror trees swap left and right relationships.

title: Invert Binary Tree
hint: Swap left and right children recursively.
intuition: Every node independently flips its children.

title: Path Sum
hint: Subtract node values from target sum while traversing.
intuition: Each recursive call reduces the remaining target.

title: Minimum Depth of Binary Tree
hint: Handle single-child nodes carefully.
intuition: Minimum depth must end at a leaf.

title: Binary Tree Paths
hint: Build path strings during DFS traversal.
intuition: Each recursive call extends the current path.

title: Diameter of Binary Tree
hint: Diameter at a node is leftHeight + rightHeight.
intuition: Longest path may pass through any node.

title: Subtree of Another Tree
hint: Compare every possible subtree root.
intuition: Subtree matching combines traversal + equality checking.

title: Count Complete Tree Nodes
hint: Perfect subtrees can be counted directly.
intuition: Complete trees have structural properties to optimize counting.

title: Binary Tree Tilt
hint: Return subtree sums while computing tilt.
intuition: Postorder traversal naturally calculates child contributions first.

title: Sum of Left Leaves
hint: Track whether current node is a left child.
intuition: Extra traversal state helps classify nodes.

title: Find Bottom Left Tree Value
hint: Track the first node of every level.
intuition: Level order traversal exposes depth layer by layer.

title: Binary Tree Maximum Path Sum
hint: Ignore negative path contributions.
intuition: Every node can become the center of the best path.

title: Binary Tree Level Order Traversal
hint: Process nodes level by level using a queue.
intuition: BFS naturally groups nodes by depth.

title: Binary Tree Zigzag Level Order Traversal
hint: Alternate traversal direction after each level.
intuition: Traversal direction can be controlled independently from BFS.

title: Binary Tree Right Side View
hint: Capture the last node of every level.
intuition: Rightmost nodes appear last during level traversal.

title: Average of Levels in Binary Tree
hint: Track level sum and node count.
intuition: BFS cleanly isolates each level.

title: Maximum Level Sum of a Binary Tree
hint: Compute sum at every level and track maximum.
intuition: Each BFS layer represents one tree depth.

title: Binary Tree Level Order Traversal II
hint: Store levels normally and reverse at the end.
intuition: Bottom-up ordering is just reversed BFS output.

title: Populating Next Right Pointers in Each Node
hint: Connect adjacent nodes within the same level.
intuition: BFS exposes natural sibling relationships.

title: Populating Next Right Pointers in Each Node II
hint: Handle missing children carefully while linking levels.
intuition: Non-perfect trees require dynamic next connections.

title: Minimum Depth of Binary Tree
hint: First leaf encountered in BFS gives minimum depth.
intuition: BFS discovers shallowest nodes first.

title: Find Largest Value in Each Tree Row
hint: Track maximum value while processing a level.
intuition: Each BFS level forms an independent group.

title: Even Odd Tree
hint: Validate parity and ordering rules at each level.
intuition: BFS cleanly separates odd and even levels.

title: Vertical Order Traversal of a Binary Tree
hint: Track row and column positions for every node.
intuition: Tree nodes can be mapped into a coordinate system.

title: Path Sum II
hint: Track current path while reducing remaining target.
intuition: Every recursive call extends the current root-to-leaf path.

title: Path Sum III
hint: Use prefix sums to count valid paths efficiently.
intuition: Current prefix sum determines previous compatible paths.

title: Sum Root to Leaf Numbers
hint: Carry current number while traversing deeper.
intuition: Every level shifts previous digits left.

title: Pseudo-Palindromic Paths in a Binary Tree
hint: Track digit frequencies along each root-to-leaf path.
intuition: Palindrome paths allow at most one odd frequency.

title: Longest Univalue Path
hint: Extend paths only through equal-valued children.
intuition: Every node can combine matching child paths.

title: House Robber III
hint: For every node, calculate rob and skip states.
intuition: Parent choice affects child availability.

title: Smallest String Starting From Leaf
hint: Build strings from leaf toward root.
intuition: Leaf paths can be compared lexicographically.

title: Delete Leaves With a Given Value
hint: Process children before deciding current node deletion.
intuition: Child deletions can turn parents into leaves.

title: Count Good Nodes in Binary Tree
hint: Track maximum value seen along the path.
intuition: A node is good if it exceeds all previous path values.

title: Distribute Coins in Binary Tree
hint: Return coin surplus or deficit upward.
intuition: Each subtree balances itself before informing parent.

title: Search in a Binary Search Tree
hint: Use BST ordering to eliminate half the tree.
intuition: Left subtree contains smaller values, right contains larger.

title: Insert into a Binary Search Tree
hint: Traverse until finding a null child position.
intuition: BST insertion preserves sorted ordering.

title: Validate Binary Search Tree
hint: Every node must stay within valid lower and upper bounds.
intuition: BST validity depends on ancestor constraints, not just parent.

title: Minimum Absolute Difference in BST
hint: Inorder traversal gives sorted values.
intuition: Closest BST values become adjacent in sorted order.

title: Kth Smallest Element in a BST
hint: Inorder traversal visits BST nodes in sorted order.
intuition: The kth inorder node is the kth smallest.

title: Convert Sorted Array to Binary Search Tree
hint: Choose middle element as root recursively.
intuition: Middle selection balances subtree heights.

title: Lowest Common Ancestor of a Binary Search Tree
hint: Move left or right depending on both target values.
intuition: BST ordering identifies subtree direction instantly.

title: Delete Node in a BST
hint: Handle leaf, one-child, and two-child cases separately.
intuition: BST deletion preserves sorted structure through replacement.

title: Trim a Binary Search Tree
hint: BST ordering lets you skip invalid subtrees entirely.
intuition: Subtrees outside the valid range can be discarded directly.

title: Convert BST to Greater Tree
hint: Traverse nodes from largest to smallest.
intuition: Reverse inorder processes bigger values first.

title: Two Sum IV - Input is a BST
hint: Track previously seen values using a set.
intuition: Tree traversal converts the problem into two-sum.

title: Recover Binary Search Tree
hint: Identify two swapped nodes during inorder traversal.
intuition: Swapped BST nodes break sorted inorder ordering.

title: Construct Binary Search Tree from Preorder Traversal
hint: Use upper bounds to decide subtree membership.
intuition: Preorder order naturally reveals subtree boundaries.

title: Balance a Binary Search Tree
hint: Store inorder traversal then rebuild balanced BST.
intuition: Sorted values can reconstruct a balanced BST.

title: Binary Search Tree Iterator
hint: Simulate inorder traversal using a stack.
intuition: Iterator lazily processes nodes in sorted order.

title: Construct Binary Tree from Preorder and Inorder Traversal
hint: Preorder gives root. Inorder splits left and right subtrees.
intuition: Traversal orders uniquely define the tree structure.

title: Construct Binary Tree from Inorder and Postorder Traversal
hint: Postorder gives the root at the end.
intuition: Inorder determines subtree boundaries while postorder identifies roots.
commonMistakes: Building left subtree before right incorrectly

title: Construct String from Binary Tree
hint: Build preorder-style representation recursively.
intuition: Tree structure can be encoded using parentheses.

title: Serialize and Deserialize Binary Tree
hint: Store null markers while traversing the tree.
intuition: Tree structure requires preserving missing-child positions.

title: Verify Preorder Serialization of a Binary Tree
hint: Track available child slots while processing nodes.
intuition: Every node consumes one slot and may create new slots.

title: Encode N-ary Tree to Binary Tree
hint: Store first child as left node and siblings through right links.
intuition: Binary trees can simulate N-ary relationships systematically.

title: Recover a Tree From Preorder Traversal
hint: Depth markers determine parent-child relationships.
intuition: Traversal depth reconstructs hierarchy structure.

title: Construct Quad Tree
hint: Split grid recursively until regions become uniform.
intuition: Quad trees recursively compress spatial regions.

title: Lowest Common Ancestor of a Binary Tree
hint: If nodes are found in different subtrees, current node becomes LCA.
intuition: The first split point during DFS is the common ancestor.

title: Step-By-Step Directions From a Binary Tree Node to Another
hint: Find paths from root to both nodes and compare prefixes.
intuition: Shared prefix reveals the common ancestor.

title: Binary Tree Paths
hint: Build path strings while traversing deeper.
intuition: Every recursive call extends the current path.

title: Longest ZigZag Path in a Binary Tree
hint: Track previous direction while traversing.
intuition: Every move alternates between left and right.

title: Longest Consecutive Sequence in Binary Tree
hint: Extend path only when child value equals parent + 1.
intuition: Current node determines whether sequence continues.

title: Leaf-Similar Trees
hint: Collect leaf values in traversal order.
intuition: Leaf sequence uniquely describes leaf traversal.

title: All Nodes Distance K in Binary Tree
hint: Store parent references to allow upward traversal.
intuition: Trees behave like undirected graphs for distance problems.

title: Smallest Subtree with all the Deepest Nodes
hint: Return both subtree depth and candidate node.
intuition: Equal subtree depths indicate ancestor convergence.

title: Path Sum IV
hint: Decode node positions before traversal.
intuition: Encoded values still preserve tree hierarchy.

title: Count Nodes Equal to Average of Subtree
hint: Return subtree sum and node count together.
intuition: Postorder traversal computes subtree statistics naturally.

title: Flatten Binary Tree to Linked List
hint: Flatten left and right subtrees before rewiring pointers.
intuition: Preorder traversal defines the linked-list order.

title: Boundary of Binary Tree
hint: Process left boundary, leaves, then reversed right boundary.
intuition: Boundary nodes belong to distinct traversal categories.

title: Binary Tree Vertical Order Traversal
hint: Track column indices during traversal.
intuition: Nodes sharing the same column belong together vertically.

title: Binary Tree Cameras
hint: Use states to represent covered and uncovered nodes.
intuition: Optimal camera placement depends on child states.

title: Count Univalue Subtrees
hint: A subtree is valid only if both children match current value.
intuition: Postorder traversal validates children before parents.

title: Maximum Width of Binary Tree
hint: Assign positional indices to nodes.
intuition: Virtual indexing preserves full binary tree positions.

title: Check Completeness of a Binary Tree
hint: After the first null node, no valid nodes should appear.
intuition: Complete trees fill levels from left to right.

title: Find Duplicate Subtrees
hint: Serialize subtrees and count occurrences.
intuition: Identical structures produce identical serializations.

title: Maximum Difference Between Node and Ancestor
hint: Track minimum and maximum ancestor values along the path.
intuition: Best difference depends on extreme ancestor values.

title: Maximum Product of Splitted Binary Tree
hint: Compute all subtree sums and maximize split product.
intuition: Every subtree edge forms a possible partition.

title: Number of Good Leaf Nodes Pairs
hint: Return leaf distances upward from each subtree.
intuition: Parent nodes combine leaf distances from both sides.

title: Count Nodes With the Highest Score
hint: Calculate subtree sizes for every node.
intuition: Removing a node splits the tree into components.

title: Lowest Common Ancestor of Deepest Leaves
hint: Return both subtree depth and candidate ancestor.
intuition: Equal subtree depths indicate the merge point of deepest leaves.

title: Smallest Common Region
hint: Store parent relationships and trace ancestors upward.
intuition: LCA logic also works on generic parent hierarchies.

title: Lowest Common Ancestor of a Binary Tree II
hint: Ensure both target nodes actually exist in the tree.
intuition: Normal LCA logic fails if one node is missing.

title: Lowest Common Ancestor of a Binary Tree III
hint: Use linked-list intersection style traversal.
intuition: Switching roots synchronizes path lengths.

title: Kth Ancestor of a Tree Node
hint: Precompute ancestors at powers of two distances.
intuition: Binary decomposition jumps upward efficiently.

title: Tree of Coprimes
hint: Track latest ancestors for every value during DFS.
intuition: Path ancestor state changes dynamically while traversing.

title: Serialize and Deserialize BST
hint: BST ordering allows reconstruction without null markers.
intuition: Value bounds uniquely determine subtree placement.

title: Recover Tree From Preorder Traversal
hint: Node depth determines parent-child relationships.
intuition: Traversal depth encodes tree hierarchy.

title: Delete Nodes And Return Forest
hint: When deleting a node, its children may become new roots.
intuition: Tree deletion can split the structure into multiple trees.

title: Maximum Sum BST in Binary Tree
hint: Return subtree min, max, sum, and BST validity together.
intuition: Parent validity depends completely on child subtree properties.

title: Longest Path With Different Adjacent Characters
hint: Combine the two longest valid child paths.
intuition: Best answer may pass through the current node.

title: Amount of Time for Binary Tree to Be Infected
hint: Convert the tree into an undirected graph.
intuition: Infection spreads in all directions like BFS on graphs.

title: Height of Binary Tree After Subtree Removal Queries
hint: Precompute subtree heights and outside heights separately.
intuition: Every node's answer depends on information outside its subtree.

title: Sum of Distances in Tree
hint: First compute subtree answers, then reroot for all nodes.
intuition: Changing the root shifts distance contributions predictably.

title: Merge BSTs to Create Single BST
hint: Leaf nodes may become attachment points for other BST roots.
intuition: Merged trees must still satisfy global BST constraints.
